pg129-p1-s2: Father feels he has struck something better than gold.
pg129-p1-s3:
For a time he may try to hug the new
treasure to himself.
pg129-p1-s4:
He may not see at once that he has barely scratched a limitless lode which will pay dividends only
if he mines it for the rest of his life and insists on giving away the entire product.
pg129-p1:
frustration knew no bounds. Father feels he has struck something better than gold. For a time he may try to hug the new
treasure to himself. He may not see at once that he has barely scratched a limitless lode which will pay dividends only
if he mines it for the rest of his life and insists on giving away the entire product.
pg129-p2-s1: If the family cooperates, dad will soon see that he is suffering from a distortion of values.
pg129-p2-s2:
He will perceive that his
spiritual growth is lopsided, that for an average man like himself, a spiritual life which does not include his family
obligations may not be so perfect after all.
pg129-p2-s3:
If the family will appreciate that dad's current behavior is but a phase of
his development, all will be well.
pg129-p2-s4:
In the midst of an understanding and sympathetic family, these vagaries of dad's
spiritual infancy will quickly disappear.
pg129-p2:
If the family cooperates, dad will soon see that he is suffering from a distortion of values. He will perceive that his
spiritual growth is lopsided, that for an average man like himself, a spiritual life which does not include his family
obligations may not be so perfect after all. If the family will appreciate that dad's current behavior is but a phase of
his development, all will be well. In the midst of an understanding and sympathetic family, these vagaries of dad's
spiritual infancy will quickly disappear.
pg129-p3-s1: The opposite may happen should the family condemn and criticize.
pg129-p3-s2:
Dad may feel that for years his drinking has placed him
on the wrong side of every argument, but that now he has become a superior person with God on his side.
pg129-p3-s3:
If the family
persists in criticism, this fallacy may take a still greater hold on father.
pg129-p3-s4:
Instead of treating the family as he
should, he may retreat further into himself and feel he has spiritual justification for so doing.
pg129-p3:
The opposite may happen should the family condemn and criticize. Dad may feel that for years his drinking has placed him
on the wrong side of every argument, but that now he has become a superior person with God on his side. If the family
persists in criticism, this fallacy may take a still greater hold on father. Instead of treating the family as he
should, he may retreat further into himself and feel he has spiritual justification for so doing.
pg129-p4-s1: Though the family does not fully agree with dad's spiritual activities, they should let him have his head.
pg129-p4-s2:
Even if he
displays a certain amount of neglect and irresponsibility towards the family, it is well to let him go as far as he
likes in helping other alcoholics.
pg129-p4-s3:
During those first days of convalescence, this will do more to insure his sobriety
than anything else.
pg129-p4:

likes in helping other alcoholics. During those first days of convalescence, this will do more to insure his sobriety
